title: aquilegia micrantha var. micrantha
intro: Aquilegia micrantha var. micrantha, also known as the small-flowered monk's hood or small-flowered columbine, is a perennial plant species native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to the presence of saponins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Aquilegia micrantha var. micrantha include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and act as an expectorant to help relieve congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the roots to create a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
